work_unit: fix H-001 commit-gate -a/--all bypass of case-metadata exemption
situation: audit-2026-07-03-017 found H-001: when is_case_metadata_only(staged) returns True, the commit is exempted from main-branch block. But git commit -a auto-stages ALL modified tracked files at commit time, bypassing the staged-only exemption check. Real gate bypass vector allowing non-metadata code to be committed directly to main.
action: Added _unstaged_modified_files() helper (mirrors staged_files but uses git diff --name-only without --cached). In the exemption path, detect -a/--all/-am flags in rest tokens; if present, also verify unstaged modified files pass is_case_metadata_only(). If any unstaged file is non-metadata, deny exemption with descriptive message. Single-file fix in autonomous-commit-gate.py.
outcome_evidence: python3 py_compile OK; opt-worktree.sh committed + pushed to origin/auto/opt-hooks-1783036663; direction_kind=route-fix (no public-interface files touched); new worktree created because area=hooks differs from prior area=engine
next_suggestion: Dispatch H-003 (check-planning-status.sh shell injection, route-fix, trivial) next — audit report recommends it as highest-impact-easiest-fix. Then M-008+M-009 atomic-write batch.

audit_findings.finding: H-001: git commit -a/--all bypasses case-metadata exemption by auto-staging unstaged modified files that are not checked by is_case_metadata_only()
audit_findings.remediation: Detect -a/--all/-am flags; when present, also check unstaged modified files against exemption set. Deny exemption if any unstaged file is non-metadata.
